issues: The first distractor treats “returns the daggers himself” as a diagnosed error, but the supplied excerpt ends when Lady Macbeth orders him to return them; it does not show Macbeth’s response or establish who returns them. That part of the choice and its feedback therefore rely on an unsupplied plot detail.
learnerValue: The repaired front is self-contained and explicitly identifies Duncan’s killing, while the core answer accurately links Macbeth’s blocked “Amen” with the accusation that he has murdered sleep.
